summary: This cluster investigates the cellular and molecular mechanisms underlying algal adaptation to environmental and physiological stress, with a focus on proteomic and ultrastructural changes. Studies on the coenocytic green alga *Bryopsis plumosa* revealed dramatic shifts in protein expression and cell wall formation during protoplast regeneration, while research on the mixotrophic dinoflagellate *Prorocentrum micans* identified unique proteins expressed under mixotrophic conditions, including those with homology to insect and bacterial genes. Both papers employed mass spectrometry-based proteomics and microscopy to document these processes, highlighting a shared emphasis on cell rebuilding and trophic mode switching. The geographic scope is not explicitly defined, but the work on *P. micans* involved association with the cryptophyte *Rhodomonas salina*, suggesting a focus on species interactions in marine or brackish environments.
